software.name: Vysor
software.purpose: See and control your Android or Inrico radio screen on your PC (helpful when the radio has no touch or a tiny screen).
software.notes: Free tier works for mirroring. Install Vysor on your PC, enable USB debugging on the device, connect USB, then click View in Vysor.

software.name: scrcpy (free alternative)
software.purpose: Lightweight screen mirror — no account required. Best for technicians comfortable with a command window.
software.notes: Download the latest scrcpy-win64-v* zip (Windows), unzip, run scrcpy.exe with the radio connected by USB.

software.name: Android Platform Tools (ADB)
software.purpose: Required for TM-7 Plus accessibility setup and advanced installs. Includes adb.exe.
software.notes: After install, open Command Prompt and run adb devices to confirm the radio appears.

software.name: safeT Mobile APK
software.purpose: The handset app itself — get the latest signed build from your agency admin.
software.notes: Agency admins: sign in to safeT Control → Downloads → Download APK. Copy the link or file to each radio.

sections.title: Before you start
sections.intro: You will need: (1) your agency slug, username, and password from your administrator, (2) the safeT Mobile APK file or download link, and (3) for Inrico radios, a USB cable to connect the radio to a Windows PC.

sections.title: Step 1 — Get the APK from your admin
sections.intro: The app is not in the Google Play Store. Your agency admin publishes the official build on your safeT server.

steps.title: Admin downloads the APK
steps.body: An agency administrator signs in to safeT Control (the admin website), opens the Downloads tab, and clicks Download APK. They can copy the download link to send to installers.
steps.imageAlt: safeT Control Downloads tab with Download APK button

steps.title: Copy the APK to the radio
steps.body: Move the APK file to the device using any method that works for you: USB cable + File Explorer, SD card, email to yourself, Google Drive, or download directly in the radio's browser using the link from Control.
steps.imageAlt: Diagram showing APK copied from PC to phone via USB or cloud

sections.title: Step 2 — Turn on Developer options & USB debugging
sections.intro: Required for Vysor/scrcpy and for TM-7 Plus hardware-key setup. Standard Android phones only need this if you are mirroring from a PC.

steps.title: Open Settings → About phone
steps.body: Scroll to the bottom of Settings and tap About phone (or About device).
steps.imageAlt: Android Settings with About phone highlighted

steps.title: Tap Build number seven times
steps.body: Find Build number and tap it quickly seven times. Android will say You are now a developer! On Inrico radios the menu may be labeled slightly differently — look for Build number or Internal version.
steps.imageAlt: About phone screen with Build number and developer enabled message

steps.title: Enable USB debugging
steps.body: Go back to Settings → System → Developer options (or Settings → Developer options). Turn on USB debugging. When you plug in USB, tap Allow on the Allow USB debugging? prompt and check Always allow from this computer.
steps.imageAlt: Developer options with USB debugging toggle on

sections.title: Step 3 — Install the APK
sections.intro: Android blocks apps from outside the Play Store until you allow installation from your file manager or browser.

steps.title: Allow installs from unknown sources
steps.body: Open the APK from Files or Downloads. If Android blocks it, go to Settings → Apps → Special access → Install unknown apps (wording varies). Enable Allow for Files, Chrome, or whichever app you use to open the APK.
steps.imageAlt: Install unknown apps permission enabled for Files

steps.title: Tap Install
steps.body: Open the APK again. Review the prompt and tap Install. When finished, tap Open — or find safeT PTT in the app drawer.
steps.imageAlt: Android package installer with Install button

sections.title: Step 4 — Sign in and allow permissions
sections.intro: The first time you open safeT Mobile, you will sign in and grant microphone, location, and notification access.

steps.title: Enter agency, username, and password
steps.body: Agency / network is your agency slug (example: metro-pd). Username and password are the radio account your admin created. Platform owner accounts cannot sign in on handsets — use the web console instead.
steps.imageAlt: safeT PTT login screen with agency slug and credentials

steps.title: Allow microphone
steps.body: Push-to-talk needs the microphone. Tap ALLOW MIC or accept the system permission dialog. Without this, transmit will not work.

steps.title: Allow location and notifications
steps.body: Location lets dispatch see your unit on the map. Notifications keep the radio connected in the background. Accept both when prompted.

sections.title: Inrico IRC590 — hardware notes
sections.intro: The IRC590 is an ultracompact rugged LTE radio. safeT Mobile auto-detects the IRC590 profile and maps hardware keys for you.

steps.title: Use the hardware PTT button
steps.body: Press and hold the orange PTT key to transmit — same as a traditional radio. Release to stop. The on-screen PTT button still works if you prefer.
steps.imageAlt: Inrico IRC590 with PTT and emergency keys labeled

steps.title: Emergency button
steps.body: The red EMERGENCY key sends a priority alert to dispatch and clears the channel. Use only for real emergencies.

steps.title: Volume check key
steps.body: Hold the volume-check side key (Inrico key 232) to play a short test tone through the speaker so field users can verify audio level.

steps.title: Optional: mirror with Vysor for first-time setup
steps.body: The IRC590 screen is small. For initial login, connect USB, open Vysor on your PC, and complete sign-in from your computer keyboard. After that, the hardware keys work without the PC.
steps.imageAlt: PC and IRC590 connected with Vysor mirroring

sections.title: Inrico TM-7 / TM-7 Plus — hardware keys & accessibility
sections.intro: The TM-7 Plus has a larger touch display, a volume knob, and programmable side keys. Physical PTT and Emergency require a one-time accessibility setup on many Android 10 units.

steps.title: Try the in-app accessibility shortcut first
steps.body: Open safeT PTT. If you see a warning about accessibility, tap OPEN ACCESSIBILITY SETTINGS and look for safeT PTT in the list. Turn it ON if it appears.
steps.imageAlt: Accessibility settings with safeT PTT service toggle

steps.title: If the list is empty (common on Android 10 TM-7 Plus)
steps.body: Inrico sometimes hides sideloaded apps from the on-screen accessibility list. Use ADB from a Windows PC instead: connect USB, confirm USB debugging is on, install Platform Tools, then run the commands below.
steps.imageAlt: TM-7 Plus connected to PC with adb devices output

steps.title: ADB commands (copy and paste)
steps.body:
Open Command Prompt on your PC. Run adb devices and accept the prompt on the radio. Then run:

adb shell settings put secure accessibility_enabled 1
adb shell settings put secure enabled_accessibility_services com.securityradio.ptt/com.securityradio.ptt.device.InricoHardwareService

If other accessibility services are already enabled, append :com.securityradio.ptt/com.securityradio.ptt.device.InricoHardwareService to the existing value instead of replacing it.
steps.code:
adb devices
adb shell settings put secure accessibility_enabled 1
adb shell settings put secure enabled_accessibility_services com.securityradio.ptt/com.securityradio.ptt.device.InricoHardwareService

steps.title: Test hardware PTT and Emergency
steps.body: Close and reopen safeT PTT. Press the hardware PTT key — you should hear the talk-permit tone and see transmitting on screen. Test the red Emergency key in a non-emergency drill channel first.

steps.title: Volume knob
steps.body: Rotate the hardware volume knob to adjust speaker level. safeT maps detents correctly on TM-7 Plus firmware.

sections.title: Enterprise mass deploy & MDM
sections.intro: Agencies with dozens or hundreds of handsets need repeatable provisioning. Today safeT uses a signed APK sideload model; full MDM auto-enrollment is in active development.

steps.title: What works today — manual rollout
steps.body:
1) Admin downloads the signed APK from Control → Downloads.

2) Create each radio account in Control → Users.

3) Deliver APK + credentials via USB, shared drive, email, or MDM file push.

4) Field tech follows this guide through install and sign-in.

For 10+ units, contact sales@safetptt.com for assisted onboarding.

steps.title: MDM file deploy (Intune, SOTI, Knox)
steps.body:
If your devices are already enrolled in an MDM, upload the safeT APK as a line-of-business app and assign it to device groups. Most MDMs require the user to approve install once unless the device is fully managed with silent-install permission.

You still configure agency slug and radio credentials on first launch — automated credential injection is part of the MDM enrollment feature we are building.

steps.title: In development — enrollment link & QR
steps.body: We are building one-step enrollment: scan a QR code or open a signed link to download the APK, pre-fill agency slug, and bind the handset in one flow. Join the MDM beta waitlist by emailing sales@safetptt.com with your MDM platform (Intune, SOTI, etc.) and fleet size.

steps.title: Stay on supported builds
steps.body: After install, safeT Mobile checks your server for new APK versions when the app opens. When MDM rollout ships, updates will support the same centralized publish path from Control → Downloads.

sections.title: Step 5 — Verify you are on the air
sections.intro: Confirm voice and GPS reach dispatch before going live.

steps.title: Key up on your assigned channel
steps.body: Select your talkgroup (example: Green 1). Hold PTT and say a radio check. Dispatch should hear you on safeT Command and see your unit on the roster.
steps.imageAlt: safeT Mobile on channel with PTT active

steps.title: Automatic updates
steps.body: Once installed, safeT Mobile checks your server for new APK versions when the app opens. Admins publish updates from Control → Downloads; radios install with your normal sideload permission.
